[Skip to content](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/amex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you/#main) ADVERTISEMENT [Guides](https://thepointsguy.com/guides/) # Amex Platinum vs. Chase Sapphire Reserve: Which premium travel card is right for you? Danyal Ahmed ![Danyal Ahmed](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2023/10/DAhmed-Headshot-bio.jpg?width=96&crop=1%3A1%2Coffset-x50%2Coffset-y50) [Danyal Ahmed](https://thepointsguy.com/author/sahmed/) Credit card writer Credit card writer Danyal Ahmed is an avid traveler, averaging about 100,000 aeronautical miles per year. He regularly applies that knowledge and his expertise in credit cards to his role as a credit card writer at TPG. Ethan Steinberg ![Ethan Steinberg](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2020/03/WechatIMG143.jpeg?width=96&crop=1%3A1%2Coffset-x50%2Coffset-y50) [Ethan Steinberg](https://thepointsguy.com/author/ethansteinberg/) Contributor Ethan's been a frequent flier for as long as he can remember, from studying abroad in Paris to moving to Shanghai after graduating college, where he served as TPG's eyes and ears for all things travel in the Asia-Pacific region. If you asked him where he wants to travel next he'd quote Anthony Bourdain and tell you "anytime I'm eating spicy noodles in a bowl, I'm happy." also contributed to this story May 04, 2026 • 16 min read Jump to section The cards we feature here are from partners who compensate us when you are approved through our site, and this may impact how or where these products appear. We don’t cover all available credit cards, but our analysis, reviews, and opinions are entirely from our editorial team. Terms apply to the offers listed on this page. Please view our [advertising policy](https://thepointsguy.com/advertising-policy/) and [product review methodology](https://thepointsguy.com/review-methodology/) for more information. ### Editor's Note This is a recurring post, regularly updated with new information and offers. Two titans come to mind when talking about the [premium card](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/battle-of-the-premium-travel-rewards-cards/) market: The [Chase Sapphire Reserve®](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/221211836?name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&pid=221211836?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=caa8318c-4e6f-40a6-b88b-eaa5c201c1ca&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) (see [rates and fees](https://thepointsguy.com/tc/221211836?pid=221211836&name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&data-testid=ratesFees)) and the [American Express Platinum Card®](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/22504813?name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&pid=22504813?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=17adae75-a6f3-4675-bb73-900918b4be8d&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c). Both cards provide top-tier [benefits](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/credit-cards-monthly-checklist/) to enhance the travel experience and useful [statement credits](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/forgettable-statement-credits-checklist/) with savings at popular merchants. But which one deserves a spot in your wallet? Here's a side-by-side comparison of all the perks that come with the Amex Platinum and Sapphire Reserve to help you make that decision. ## Amex Platinum vs. Sapphire Reserve comparison | **Card** | **Amex Platinum** | **Sapphire Reserve** | | --- | --- | --- | | **Welcome offer** | Find out your offer and see if you are eligible for as high as 175,000 bonus points after spending $12,000 on purchases within the first six months of card membership. (Welcome offers vary, and you may not be eligible for an offer.) | Earn 150,000 bonus points after spending $6,000 on purchases in the first three months from account opening. | | **Annual fee** | $895 (see [rates and fees](https://thepointsguy.com/tc/22504813?pid=22504813&name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&data-testid=ratesFees)) | $795 | | **Earning rates** | - 5 points per dollar spent on flights booked directly with airlines or via American Express Travel® (on up to $500,000 of airfare purchases per calendar year, then 1 point per dollar spent)
- 5 points per dollar spent on prepaid hotels booked with Amex Travel
- 1 point per dollar spent on other eligible purchases | - 10 points per dollar spent on Peloton equipment and accessory purchases of $150 or more (through Dec. 31, 2027)
- 8 points per dollar spent on all Chase Travel℠ bookings, including The Edit
- 4 points per dollar spent on flights and hotels booked directly
- 3 points per dollar spent on dining
- 1 point per dollar spent on all other purchases | | **Notable benefits** | - **Hotel**: Up to $600 annual hotel statement credit per calendar year (up to $300 biannually), valid on prepaid Amex Fine Hotels + Resorts or The Hotel Collection bookings (of two nights or more) when you pay with your Amex Platinum; cardmembers also get complimentary Gold status with Hilton Honors and Marriott Bonvoy as well as complimentary Leaders Club Sterling Status from The Leading Hotels of the World\*
- **Resy:** Up to $400 annual Resy statement credit per calendar year (up to $100 quarterly) for dining at eligible U.S. restaurants\*
- **Digital streaming**: Up to $300 digital entertainment statement credit per calendar year (up to $25 monthly)\*
- **Lululemon:** Up to $300 annual statement credit for Lululemon purchases (up to $75 quarterly) at U.S. stores (excluding outlets) and online\*
- **Clear+**: Up to $209 Clear+ statement credit per calendar year to cover your annual membership (subject to auto-renewal)\*
- **Airline credit**: Up to $200 annual airline incidental statement credit with your selected airline per calendar year\*
- **Global Entry/TSA PreCheck:** Receive a $120 statement credit for Global Entry every four years or an up to $85 statement credit for TSA PreCheck every 4½ years\*
- **Oura Ring:** Up to $200 annual statement credit per calendar year (on hardware purchases only)\*
- **Uber**: Up to $200 in annual Uber Cash for U.S. services per calendar year (disbursed as $15 monthly, with a $20 bonus in December; Uber Cash will only be deposited into one Uber account when you add the Amex Platinum Card as a payment method and redeem with any Amex card)
- **Uber One:** Up to a $120 statement credit that fully covers an auto-renewing $96 annual or $9.99 monthly membership\*
- **Walmart+**: Up to $155 statement credit per calendar year to cover the cost of one monthly Walmart+ membership when paying with your Amex Platinum (up to $12.95 plus applicable taxes monthly; subject to auto-renewal; Plus Up benefits are excluded)\* | - **Hotel**: Up to $500 in credits each year for prepaid The Edit hotel bookings (of two nights or more), given as two up to $250 credits, plus a one-time $250 credit for select prepaid hotel stays booked through Chase Travel (through Dec. 31)
- **Travel**: $300 annual travel credit
- **Dining credit**: $300 annual dining credit with the Sapphire Reserve Exclusive Tables program (up to $150 biannually)
- **Entertainment**: $300 annual statement credit for StubHub or Viagogo (up to $150 biannually; through Dec. 31, 2027; activation required)
- **Apple TV+/Apple Music**: Up to $288 annually in complimentary subscriptions to Apple TV+ and Apple Music (through June 22, 2027; one-time, individual activation required)
- **DoorDash**: Complimentary DoorDash DashPass subscription (activate by Dec. 31, 2027) and up to $25 in DoorDash promos each month when you have an active DashPass membership (through Dec. 31, 2027)
- **Global Entry, TSA PreCheck or Nexus**: Up to $120 application fee credit every four years
- **Lyft**: Up to $120 annual (up to $10 monthly) credit in-app (does not apply to Wait & Save, bike or scooter rides; through Sept. 30, 2027)
- **Peloton**: Up to $120 for a Peloton membership credit (up to $10 monthly; through Dec. 31, 2027; activation required)
- **IHG elite status**: Complimentary IHG One Rewards Platinum Elite status (through Dec. 31, 2027) | _\*Enrollment required_ Get updates on special offers and compare our editors’ top credit card picks with our daily newsletter. ## Amex Platinum vs. Sapphire Reserve welcome offer When considering a new premium card with a high annual fee, a large welcome offer will help you recoup some of that cost. With the [Amex Platinum](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/22504813?name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&pid=22504813?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=54692335-7628-44f3-bfdb-9c00f2b8eecb&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c), new cardmembers can find out their offer to see if they are eligible for as high as 175,000 bonus points after spending $12,000 on purchases within the first six months of card membership. Welcome offers vary, and you may not be eligible for an offer. The Amex Platinum bonus is worth up to $3,500 based on TPG's May 2026 [valuations](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/monthly-valuations/). Meanwhile, new [Sapphire Reserve](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/221211836?name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&pid=221211836?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=c8a29dfa-515a-4680-b0ad-649a0a8bbfa0&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) cardholders can earn 150,000 bonus points after spending $6,000 on purchases in the first three months from account opening. The Sapphire Reserve bonus is worth $3,075, per our May 2026 [valuations](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/monthly-valuations/). ![](https://thepointsguy.com/images/EmailSignUp/daily-dt.svg) Daily Newsletter Reward your inbox with the TPG Daily newsletter Join over 1,000,000 readers for breaking news, in-depth guides and exclusive deals from TPG’s experts Email address Sign up By signing up, you will receive newsletters and promotional content and agree to our [Terms of Use](https://thepointsguy.com/terms-of-use/) and acknowledge the data practices in our [Privacy Policy](https://thepointsguy.com/privacy-policy/). You may unsubscribe at any time. It's worth noting that the Sapphire Reserve's bonus is easier to attain thanks to its lower spending requirement when compared to the Amex Platinum's. ![Chase Sapphire Reserve versus The Platinum Card](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2025/09/Chase-Sapphire-Reserve-and-American-Express-Platinum.jpg?width=3840) THE POINTS GUY Keep bonus eligibility for both cards in mind. - Amex only allows you to earn a welcome offer on a card [once per lifetime](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/credit-card-application-restrictions/), so if you aren't targeted for the highest offer, it may be worth holding off until you are. - Chase also [limits bonus eligibility](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/chase-sapphire-reserve-bonus-eligibility/). If you've previously received a Sapphire Reserve welcome offer, regardless of how much time has passed since you were last approved, you more than likely aren't eligible to receive another welcome bonus on that card. ![Card Illustration]() ![Person using CardMatch]() CardMatch Let us help find the best card for you Get personalized card matches to maximize your earning potential. Let's get started. How do you want to redeem your rewards? Booking travel and travel experiences Cash back and statement credits Take me to CardMatch **Winner**: Amex Platinum, if you're able to receive the highest targeted offer. **Related: [The best credit card welcome bonuses](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/best-welcome-offers/)** ## Amex Platinum vs. Sapphire Reserve benefits The [Sapphire Reserve](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/221211836?name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&pid=221211836?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=5dcc135f-b7ef-4e48-8b20-a8b2b2f4c097&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) and the [Amex Platinum](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/22504813?name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&pid=22504813?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=d7a23e8f-6455-417b-a533-80620163dffe&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) are two of the most expensive products in the card market. ![Friends looking at a phone](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2024/06/Groupof-friends-traveling-to-Berlin-Germany-looking-at-smart-phone_Hinterhaus-Productions.jpg?width=3840) HINTERHAUS PRODUCTIONS/GETTY IMAGES Despite such high annual fees, each card provides benefits that help you recoup more than you're paying. Here's how each card's benefits compare. ### Travel benefits Both cards provide similar travel perks, including hotel statement credits and membership credits for travel programs like [Global Entry](https://thepointsguy.com/travel/things-to-know-global-entry/) and [TSA PreCheck](https://thepointsguy.com/news/is-tsa-precheck-worth-it/). ![Couple traveling in Kasmil](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2024/06/Kasmil-area-of-the-Albanian-Riviera_AscentXmedia.jpg?width=3840) Kasmil area of the Albanian Riviera. ASCENTXMEDIA/GETTY IMAGES The tie-breaker here, however, is the Sapphire Reserve's easy-to-use $300 [travel credit](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/chase-sapphire-reserve-annual-travel-credit/) — especially compared to the more restrictive perks of the Amex Platinum. The Amex Platinum provides complimentary Gold status in [Hilton Honors](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/what-is-hilton-elite-status-worth/) and [Marriott Bonvoy](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/what-is-marriott-elite-status-worth/), plus [Leaders Club Sterling Status](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/leaders-club-sterling-amex-platinum-benefit/) from The Leading Hotels of the World (enrollment required). The Sapphire Reserve provides complimentary [IHG One Rewards](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/what-is-ihg-elite-status-worth/) Platinum Elite status (through Dec. 31, 2027). Plus, if you spend at least $75,000 on your Sapphire Reserve in a previous calendar year, you can unlock: - IHG One Rewards Diamond Elite Status - [World of Hyatt Explorist status](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/chase-hyatt-expand-benefits-sapphire-cardholders/) Then there's the airport lounge access offered by both cards. Eligible Amex Platinum cardmembers receive access to the [American Express Global Lounge Collection](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/american-express-platinum-lounge-access/), including [Centurion Lounges](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/the-ultimate-guide-to-amex-centurion-lounges/), Priority Pass™ lounges, [Delta Sky Clubs](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/ultimate-guide-delta-sky-club-access/) (when traveling on same-day Delta Air Lines flights; limited to 10 visits) and more (enrollment may be required). Cardmembers can unlock Centurion Lounge guest privileges after spending $75,000 in a calendar year. ![Centurion Lounge Tokyo Haneda](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2025/07/HND-Centurion-Lounge-3.jpg?width=3840) AMERICAN EXPRESS The Sapphire Reserve provides access to [Priority Pass lounges](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/all-about-priority-pass-program/) and [Chase Sapphire Lounge by The Club](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/chase-sapphire-airport-lounge/) locations, and lets you bring in up to two guests for no additional fee or minimum spending requirement. Cardholders also get [access to select Maple Leaf Lounges and Air Canada Cafes](https://thepointsguy.com/news/chase-sapphire-reserve-expands-lounge-access/) in Canada, Europe and the U.S. when traveling on a Star Alliance airline. Despite its smaller footprint, Sapphire Lounges could be more preferable for those who travel with companions since you can bring up to two guests into the lounge, whereas you'd have to reach a minimum spending requirement on the Amex Platinum to earn guest privileges. **Winner:** Sapphire Reserve. This is due to its approachable statement credits and guest privileges at Sapphire Lounges. ### Dining benefits Premium cards also provide cardmembers with benefits at home, including food delivery and dining perks. The [Sapphire Reserve's](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/221211836?name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&pid=221211836?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=f7af2513-2fd3-4f5b-831d-1605321772d1&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) dining-related perks include up to a $300 annual dining credit at [Sapphire Reserve Exclusive Tables](https://thepointsguy.com/news/chase-sapphire-reserve-dining-program/) via OpenTable. The [Amex Platinum](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/22504813?name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&pid=22504813?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=a185cbdf-ce02-4a19-b23e-d824e5f7463d&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) provides an up to $400 [Resy statement credit](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/resy-credit-guide/) (up to $100 each quarter) per calendar year for dining at eligible U.S. restaurants and access to special reservations at highly acclaimed participating restaurants through Platinum Nights by Resy (enrollment required). ![Dining out with friends](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2024/07/American-Express-Gold-Card_RESY_GOLDCARD_0231.jpg?width=3840) MEREDITH JENKS/AMERICAN EXPRESS By comparison, the Resy credit can be used at over 10,000 restaurants in the U.S., whereas the Sapphire Reserve's $300 credit is valid only at select OpenTable restaurants in roughly 50 U.S. cities. On a positive note, neither the Amex Platinum nor the Sapphire Reserve requires a reservation to use the dining credits. **Winner:** Amex Platinum. It has a higher dining statement credit value and is available at more restaurants across the U.S. ### Entertainment and lifestyle benefits The Amex Platinum's benefits extend into lifestyle perks. As noted above, cardmembers earn statement credits with notable brands such as up to $75 a quarter at [Lululemon](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/top-items-to-buy-amex-platinum-lululemon-credit/) in-store (U.S.) and online (excluding outlets), up to $200 each calendar year for an [Oura Ring](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/amex-platinum-oura-ring/) (hardware only), up to $120 annually for an [Uber One](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/credit-cards-uber-one-membership/) membership (subject to auto-renewal) and up to $12.95 a month (plus applicable taxes) for one [Walmart+ membership](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/maximize-walmart-plus-benefits-amex-platinum/) (subject to auto-renewal; Plus-Ups excluded). Additional lifestyle statement credits include an up to $300 [Equinox](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/guide-new-amex-platinum-equinox-credit/) statement credit (subject to auto-renewal) and an up to $300 SoulCycle statement credit. Enrollment is required for select benefits. ![People doing yoga on pool deck](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2025/01/20250115_Amex-Consumer-Delta-Winter-Travel_Delta-SkyMiles-Reserve-American-Express-Card_DeltaAmex_Consumer_Yoga_080_B.jpg?width=3840) DELTA AMEX While Amex overwhelms with niche statement credits, Chase keeps it simple and provides up to a $300 annual statement credit for [StubHub or Viagogo](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/chase-sapphire-reserve-lifestyle-credits/) (activation required; through Dec. 31, 2027) and complimentary memberships for [Apple TV+ and Apple Music](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/chase-sapphire-reserve-apple-tv-credit/) (a one-time activation required per service; through June 22, 2027). Additionally, Amex provides up to $25 in monthly statement credits for [streaming services](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/credit-cards-free-streaming-subscriptions/) (enrollment required). Amex Platinum is also the king of streaming service providers because, as Walmart+ members, cardmembers get access to either Paramount+ Essential (separate enrollment required) or a [Peacock Premium plan](https://thepointsguy.com/news/amex-perks-new-peacock-subscription-walmart-plus/). The Amex Platinum clearly offers more lifestyle-oriented statement credits, but many of these credits don't mean much to every cardmember, and cardmembers wind up going out of their way to use them just because the card offers them. Otherwise, these benefits are generally not on their radar. **Winner:** Tie. If the Amex Platinum statement credits apply to you, it's worth it; otherwise, the Sapphire Reserve credits are easier to use. ### Travel protections The [Sapphire Reserve](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/221211836?name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&pid=221211836?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=c077cc5d-2a51-4b82-bb83-778a5a14e668&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) comes with its industry-leading [travel protections](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/proof-needed-chases-trip-insurance/): - [Baggage delay insurance](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/which-credit-cards-cover-baggage-delays/) - [Emergency medical](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/whats-covered-by-credit-card-travel-accident-and-emergency-evacuation-insurance/) and dental benefits - [Primary car rental insurance](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/chase-sapphire-preferred-rental-car-insurance/) - [Trip cancellation/interruption insurance](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/credit-card-travel-insurance-scenarios/) - [Trip delay insurance](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/remember-these-things-trip-delay-reimbursement-credit-card/) ![Newark Airport travelers face delays](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2025/07/flight-delays-board.jpg?width=3840) An airport flight departures board at Newark Liberty International Airport (EWR). Chase has historically been the leader in travel insurance, thanks to its generous terms. Amex has [partially closed the gap](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/travel-insurance-csr-amex-platinum/), adding a suite of [travel protection benefits](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/american-express-cards-travel-protections/) to the [Amex Platinum Card](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/22504813?name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&pid=22504813?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=5b8fb5ae-aece-400c-a902-62fd84253c5b&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) **Winner:** Sapphire Reserve. It offers better overall coverage. **Related: [How to decide if a credit card's annual fee is worth paying](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/annual-fee-worth-it/)** ## Earning points on the Amex Platinum vs. Sapphire Reserve Although the welcome offer is a good way to start your points balance, you want a card that'll help you quickly earn [valuable transferable points](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/a-guide-to-earning-transferable-points/). The [Amex Platinum](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/22504813?name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&pid=22504813?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=fa139ee4-0859-4676-ba56-c7510332361e&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) earns: - 5 points per dollar spent on airfare purchased directly with airlines or through Amex Travel (on up to $500,000 of airfare purchases per calendar year, then 1 point per dollar spent) - 5 points per dollar spent on prepaid hotels booked with Amex Travel - 1 point per dollar spent on all other purchases ![hotel cafe royal lobby](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2025/04/20250408_Hotel-Cafe-Royal_Hotel-Cafe-Royal-lobby-busy_ERosen_57.jpg?width=3840) ERIC ROSEN/THE POINTS GUY The [Sapphire Reserve's](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/221211836?name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&pid=221211836?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=86179081-f8d6-4225-a81c-ced0d7bd920f&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) offers a broader range of bonus categories and earns: - 10 points per dollar spent on Peloton equipment and accessory purchases of $150 or more (through Dec. 31, 2027), with a maximum of 50,000 points - 8 points per dollar spent on all Chase Travel purchases, including The Edit - 4 points per dollar spent on flights and hotel purchases made directly with the merchant - 3 points per dollar spent on dining purchases - 1 point per dollar spent on all other purchases The Amex Platinum is best for those with significant airfare purchases (as it provides a terrific 10% return based on TPG's May 2026 valuations), but that's also where the problem lies — it's too focused on airfare purchases. The Sapphire Reserve is a card applicable to most people as it provides a more than 16% return on Chase Travel bookings (based on TPG's May 2026 valuations). It also offers more bonus categories for earning, such as dining and hotels booked directly. **Winner**: Sapphire Reserve. This card offers the best bonus points across several bonus categories. **Related: [Best rewards credit cards](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/best-travel-cards-bonus-category/)** ## Redeeming points on the Amex Platinum vs. Sapphire Reserve [Chase Ultimate Rewards](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/chase-ultimate-rewards/) and [Amex Membership Rewards](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/ultimate-guide-amex-membership-rewards/) are similarly valued at 2.05 and 2 cents apiece, respectively, according to TPG's May 2026 valuations. Chase provides an [additional bonus](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/using-chase-points-boost-hotels/) for booking through the Chase Travel portal, thanks to [Points Boost](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/chase-points-boost/). Add this to Chase's list of [its 13 airline and hotel transfer partners](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/chase-ultimate-rewards-transfer-partners/), and you have many options to choose from. ![Woman using a laptop](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2023/05/woman-making-online-purcahse-with-credit-card-and-laptop-at-cafe_Oleg-Breslavtsev.jpg?width=3840) OLEG BRESLAVTSEV/GETTY IMAGES Meanwhile, the Amex Platinum offers more ways to use your points thanks to [17 airline and three hotel transfer partners](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/membership-rewards-partner-guide/). Unfortunately, compared to Chase, American Express Travel redemptions are only 1 cent per point for flight bookings and 0.7 cents per point for hotel reservations. Points earned from the Amex Platinum and the Sapphire Reserve can be redeemed for nontravel redemptions such as cash back, statement credits and gift cards, but these redemptions generally yield poor value. **Winner**: Sapphire Reserve. The card has a higher redemption value for travel. ## Transferring points on the Amex Platinum vs. Sapphire Reserve You'll almost always get better value whenever [transferring your points](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/sweet-spots-chase-ultimate-rewards/) to airline and hotel loyalty programs. All Chase partner transfers are at a 1:1 ratio, and [most of them are instant](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/ultimate-rewards-transfer-times/). An especially valuable transfer partner is [World of Hyatt](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/ultimate-guide-world-of-hyatt/). On the airline side, popular transfer partners include [Air France-KLM Flying Blue](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/ultimate-guide-flying-blue/), [Southwest Rapid Rewards](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/southwest-rapid-rewards/) and [Virgin Atlantic Flying Club](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/great-value-virgin-atlantic-flying-club/). ![Person holding credit card while online shopping](https://runway-media-production.global.ssl.fastly.net/us/originals/2023/04/Person-holding-credit-cared-at-home-using-laptop_Rockaa.jpg?width=3840) ROCKAA/GETTY IMAGES Meanwhile, Amex Membership Rewards has 20 transfer partners, though not all are worthwhile. Some transfer ratios are below 1:1, and [some transfer times are longer](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/american-express-membership-rewards-transfer-times/), which can be risky, as award space may disappear while you wait. I have both the [Amex Platinum](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/22504813?name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&pid=22504813?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=63948c6d-0776-46e8-b20e-c423913ebf2b&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) and the [Sapphire Reserve](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/221211836?name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&pid=221211836?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=1160bb36-07bc-441b-bf08-7923da91ed9e&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c)and prefer transferring my points to programs like [Air Canada Aeroplan](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/air-canada-aeroplan/), [ANA Mileage Club](https://thepointsguy.com/guide/ana-mileage-club-guide/) and [British Airways Club](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/ultimate-ba-avios-guide/). For example, I transferred points from my Sapphire Reserve to Aeroplan to book a multicity stopover ticket in business class throughout Europe for my family. Due to higher summer pricing, the cost would've been well over $2,000 if I'd paid cash for the flights. **Winner**: Tie. Although the Sapphire Reserve has better transfer ratios, the Amex Platinum has more partners, including some not found with other issuers. **Related: [A complete list of transfer bonuses over the past decade — which issuer is the most generous?](https://thepointsguy.com/loyalty-programs/complete-history-credit-card-transfer-bonuses/)** ## Should I get the Amex Platinum or Sapphire Reserve? Choose the Amex Platinum if you maximize credits and luxury perks; choose the Sapphire Reserve if you value flexibility and hassle-free rewards. The Amex Platinum is ideal for frequent flyers who want premium travel benefits, elite hotel status and a wide range of lifestyle credits that can easily outweigh the annual fee when fully used. The Sapphire Reserve is better for travelers who want simplicity, flexible redemptions and strong everyday earning on dining, travel and Chase Travel bookings. Its easy-to-use $300 annual travel credit and industry-leading travel protections make it the more practical premium card for most people. ## Bottom line If your lifestyle revolves around high-end travel, frequent flights and you love using statement credits for hotel stays, dining, streaming and memberships, the [Amex Platinum](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/22504813?name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&pid=22504813?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=9d9dd164-298f-4ac6-88fb-61d054e4cd05&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) can offer massive value. But if you prefer a simpler, more versatile rewards structure that works reliably for flights, hotels, dining and travel — with fewer hoops and broader redemption flexibility — the [Sapphire Reserve](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/221211836?name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&pid=221211836?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=2fd0a411-6a77-4801-ad09-71025b73f8c1&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c) is the more pragmatic choice. If you travel often and spend heavily, combining both can maximize benefits. But for most people, the Reserve will deliver the best balance of rewards, flexibility and ease. To learn more, read our full reviews of the [Amex Platinum](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/reviews/amex-platinum-review/) and the [Sapphire Reserve](https://thepointsguy.com/credit-cards/reviews/chase-sapphire-reserve-review/). * * * **Apply here: [Amex Platinum](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/22504813?name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&pid=22504813?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=00eb1a21-6876-4521-815d-dd2e3cdfb20e&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c)** **Apply here: [Chase Sapphire Reserve](https://thepointsguy.com/oc/221211836?name=Chase%20Sapphire%20Reserve%20PID&pid=221211836?tid=post&c_anonymous_id=13fdb46f-5be7-42d9-bfae-64ce9b28c0fd&c_correlation_id=3d631b11-3107-4e0e-bff6-a5fdcae695d3&c_exitPageId=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_instance_id=4458ad7c-5f7b-4ef7-80ad-f8c500a3eb70&c_landing_page_url=thepointsguy.com%2Fcredit-cards%2Famex-platinum-vs-chase-sapphire-reserve-which-card-is-right-for-you%2F&c_session_id=f5919955-e4b2-4270-b2fd-5b35e4069c66&c_tenant_id=8830ce36-13f9-40da-8176-b8cbf73a598c)** * * * _For rates and fees of the Amex Platinum, click [here](https://thepointsguy.com/tc/22504813?pid=22504813&name=American%20Express%20Platinum%20Card%C2%AE&data-testid=ratesFees)._ Editorial disclaimer: Opinions expressed here are the author’s alone, not those of any bank, credit card issuer, airline or hotel chain, and have not been reviewed, approved or otherwise endorsed by any of these entities. ##
